description_long: This object is a pan flute, composed of a series of cylindrical bamboo-like tubes of varying lengths, arranged in a gentle arc and bound together by a horizontal band. Each tube is hollow, open at the top, and closed at the bottom, allowing for the production of different musical notes when air is blown across the openings. The tubes are made of a wood-toned material, giving the instrument a natural, earthy appearance. The binding band appears to be a darker wood or leather, tightly securing the tubes in place. The surface of the tubes is smooth with a subtle sheen, and there are no visible decorative engravings or embellishments. The overall craftsmanship suggests a traditional, hand-made design typical of Andean or folk instruments.
description: A traditional pan flute made of smooth, wood-toned tubes of varying lengths, bound together in an arc by a darker band. The instrument has a natural, handcrafted appearance with no visible decorations.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full arc of the pan flute and the open tops of all tubes.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the instrument and the staggered lengths of the tubes.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the closed bottoms of the tubes and the binding band.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the arc and the varying tube heights.
